seoTitle: File Taxes for Free on TurboTax (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to file your taxes for free on TurboTax by understanding eligibility requirements and navigating the platform step by step.
intro: Not everyone realizes they may qualify to file their taxes at no cost, and understanding TurboTax's free filing option can save real money. This guide covers how to file taxes for free on TurboTax, from setting up your account to submitting your return. It's aimed at students, freelancers, and anyone with a relatively simple tax situation.

steps.heading: Confirm your eligibility
steps.body: Check whether your income and tax situation qualify for TurboTax's free filing tier before starting.

steps.heading: Set up your TurboTax account
steps.body: Create or log into your TurboTax account and select the free filing option if you're eligible.

steps.heading: Import your financial documents
steps.body: Use TurboTax's import tools to bring in W-2s and other tax documents automatically where possible.

steps.heading: Review deductions and credits
steps.body: Check for any deductions or credits you may qualify for, even within the free filing tier.

steps.heading: Submit your return
steps.body: Once everything is entered and reviewed, file your return electronically through TurboTax.

faq.question: Who qualifies for free filing on TurboTax?
faq.answer: Eligibility generally depends on your income level and the complexity of your tax situation, such as having only W-2 income.

faq.question: Can self-employed individuals file for free on TurboTax?
faq.answer: Typically no — self-employment income usually requires a paid tier due to the additional forms involved.

faq.question: Does free filing include state tax returns?
faq.answer: This varies, so it's worth confirming whether your state return is included in the free tier before starting.

faq.question: Will TurboTax try to upsell me during free filing?
faq.answer: It's common to see prompts for paid features, but you can typically decline them and continue with the free option.
